message:
fix(fp-frame): Expanding mean/min/max empty-window NaN parity (frankenpandas-3kew)

When Expanding is configured with min_periods=0, the aggregation
function is invoked on an empty slice for every position where no
non-missing values have been seen yet. Prior behavior:

- mean(): returned 0.0 / 0.0 = NaN by accident (works out, but
  undocumented and non-local).
- min(): returned f64::INFINITY from the empty-fold identity.
- max(): returned f64::NEG_INFINITY.

pandas emits NaN for all three empty-window cases. Explicit
is_empty guards now return f64::NAN before the fold runs, so the
emitted Scalar::Float64(NaN) is_missing()→true without surprising
infinities leaking into downstream arithmetic.

sum() is left as identity-0.0 on empty slices — that's pandas'
empty-sum convention and only kicks in when the caller explicitly
requests min_periods=0 (otherwise the apply_expanding guard
short-circuits to Null(NaN) anyway).

Three tests: expanding_mean_empty_window_is_nan_not_zero,
expanding_sum_empty_window_returns_zero_when_min_periods_zero,
expanding_min_max_empty_window_is_nan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
